title: Convert units of time
description: Convert lengths of time between seconds, minutes, and hours
topic_path: Module 2: Multi-digit whole number and decimal fraction operations
Topic D: Measurement word problems with whole number and decimal multiplication

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table to convert the units of time from  hours to minutes or minutes to hours.**

Minutes | Hours
:-: | :-:
$60$ | $1$
$300$ |  [[☃ input-number 5]]
[[☃ input-number 6]] | $4$ 

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints:
$1 \text{ hour} ={60}\text{ minutes}$

So, $1 \text{ minute} = \dfrac1{{60}}\text{ hour}$.

Let's convert  ${300}$ minutes to hours.

$\begin{align}
{300} \text{ minutes} &=  {300}\text{ minutes}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ hour}}{{60}\text{ minutes}}} \\\\
&=  {300}~\cancel{\text{ minutes}}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ hour}}{{60}~\cancel{\text{ minutes}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{300}{60}\text{ hours}\\\\
&={300} \div {60\text{ hours}}\\\\
&={5}\text{ hours}\\\\
\end{align}$

Here is the completed table:

Minutes | Hours
:-: | :-:
$60$ | $1$
$300$ | $\blueD5$
$\maroonD{240}$ | $4$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table to convert the units of time from  hours to minutes or minutes to hours.**

Minutes | Hours
:-: | :-:
$60$ | $1$
$180$ |  [[☃ input-number 7]]
[[☃ input-number 8]] | $6$



[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints:
$1 \text{ hour} ={60}\text{ minutes}$

So, $1 \text{ minute} = \dfrac1{{60}}\text{ hour}$.

Let's convert  ${180}$ minutes to hours.

$\begin{align}
{180} \text{ minutes} &=  {180}\text{ minutes}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ hour}}{{60}\text{ minutes}}} \\\\
&=  {180}~\cancel{\text{ minutes}}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ hour}}{{60}~\cancel{\text{ minutes}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{180}{60}\text{ hours}\\\\
&={180} \div {60\text{ hours}}\\\\
&={3}\text{ hours}\\\\
\end{align}$

Here is the completed table:

Minutes | Hours
:-: | :-:
$60$ | $1$
$180$ | $\blueD3$
$\maroonD{360}$ | $6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table to convert the units of time from  minutes to seconds or seconds to minutes.**

Seconds | Minutes
:-: | :-:
$60$ | $1$
$120$ | [[☃ input-number 3]]
[[☃ input-number 4]] | $7$

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints:
$1 \text{ minute} ={60}\text{ seconds}$

So, $1 \text{ second} = \dfrac1{{60}}\text{ minutes}$.

Let's convert  ${120}$ seconds to minutes.

$\begin{align}
{120} \text{ seconds} &=  {120}\text{ seconds}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ minute}}{{60}\text{ seconds}}} \\\\
&=  {120}~\cancel{\text{ seconds}}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ minute}}{{60}~\cancel{\text{ seconds}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{120}{60}\text{ minutes}\\\\
&={120} \div {60\text{ minutes}}\\\\
&={2}\text{ minutes}\\\\
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table to convert the units of time from  minutes to seconds or seconds to minutes.**

Seconds | Minutes
:-: | :-:
$60$ | $1$
$240$ | [[☃ input-number 3]]
[[☃ input-number 4]] | $5$


[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints:
$1 \text{ minute} ={60}\text{ seconds}$

So, $1 \text{ second} = \dfrac1{{60}}\text{ minutes}$.

Let's convert  ${240}$ seconds to minutes.

$\begin{align}
{240} \text{ seconds} &=  {240}\text{ seconds}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ minute}}{{60}\text{ seconds}}} \\\\
&=  {240}~\cancel{\text{ seconds}}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ minute}}{{60}~\cancel{\text{ seconds}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{240}{60}\text{ minutes}\\\\
&={240} \div {60\text{ minutes}}\\\\
&={4}\text{ minutes}\\\\
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert $360$ seconds to minutes.**

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes

[[☃ explanation 3]]
hints:
$1 \text{ minute} = 60\text{ seconds}$

So, $1 \text{ second} = \dfrac1{60}\text{ minute}$.

Let's convert  $360$ seconds to minutes.

$\begin{align}
{360} \text{ seconds} &=  {360}\text{ seconds}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ minute}}{{60}\text{ seconds}}} \\\\
&=  {360}~\cancel{\text{ seconds}}\times{\dfrac{1\text{ minute}}{{60}~\cancel{\text{ seconds}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{360}{60}\text{ minutes}\\\\
&={360} \div {60\text{ minutes}}\\\\
&={6}\text{ minutes}\\\\
\end{align}$
$360$ seconds converts to $6$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert $\dfrac{1}{4}$ minute to seconds.**

[[☃ input-number 1]] seconds

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints:
$1 \text{ minute} = 60\text{ seconds}$

So, $1 \text{ second} = \dfrac1{60}\text{ minute}$.

Let's convert $\dfrac{1}{4}$ minute into seconds.

$\begin{align}
{\dfrac{1}{4}}\text{ minute}&={\dfrac{1}{4}}\text{ minute}\times\dfrac{60\text{ seconds}}{1\text{ minute}}\\\\
&={\dfrac{1}{4}}\cancel{\text{ minute}}\times\dfrac{60\text{ seconds}}{1\cancel{\text{ minute}}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{60\text{ seconds}}{4}\\\\
&=60\text{ seconds} \div 4\\\\
&={15}\text{ seconds}
\end{align}$
$\dfrac14$ minute converts to $15$ seconds.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert $\dfrac{1}{3}$ hour to minutes.**


[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints:
$1 \text{ hour} ={60}\text{ minutes}$

So, $1 \text{ minute} = \dfrac1{{60}}\text{ hour}$.

Let's convert $\dfrac{1}{3}$ hour into minutes.

$\begin{align}
{\dfrac{1}{3}}\text{ hour}&={\dfrac{1}{3}}\text{ hour}\times\dfrac{60\text{ minutes}}{1\text{ hour}}\\\\
&={\dfrac{1}{3}}\cancel{\text{ hour}}\times\dfrac{60\text{ minutes}}{1\cancel{\text{ hour}}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{60\text{ minutes}}{3}\\\\
&=60\text{ minutes} \div 3\\\\
&={20}\text{ minutes}
\end{align}$
$\dfrac{1}{3}$ hour converts to $20$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert $5$ hours to seconds.**


[[☃ input-number 1]] seconds

[[☃ explanation 2]]
hints: $5$ hours converts to $18{,}000$ seconds.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

hints: Let's convert $\blueD1\dfrac{\blueD1}\blueD{2}$ hours to minutes before we compare.
$1 \text{ hour} ={60}\text{ minutes}$

$\begin{align}
\blueD1\dfrac{\blueD1}\blueD{2} \text{ hours} &= {\dfrac{3}{2}}\text{ hours}\\\\
&= {\dfrac{3}{2}}\text{ hours} \times \dfrac{60\text{ minutes}}{1\text{ hour}}\\\\
&= {\dfrac{3}{2}}\cancel{\text{ hours}} \times \dfrac{60\text{ minutes}}{1\cancel{\text{ hour}}}\\\\
&={\dfrac{{180\text{ minutes}}}{2}}\\\\
&={180\text{ minutes}} \div {2}\\\\
&=\blueD{90}\text{ minutes}\\\\
\end{align}$

Now that both measures are in minutes, we can compare them.

Which is larger? $\blueD{90}$ minutes or $\greenD{100}$ minutes?
$\blueD{90}\text{ minutes} < \greenD{100}\text{ minutes}$
$\blueD1 \dfrac\blueD1\blueD2\text {hours}<$  $\greenD{100} \text{ minutes}$

hints: Let's convert $\blueD2\dfrac{\blueD1}\blueD{2}$ minutes to seconds before we compare.
$1 \text{ minute} ={60}\text{ seconds}$

$\begin{align}
\blueD2\dfrac{\blueD1}\blueD{2} \text{ minutes} &= {\dfrac{5}{2}}\text{ minutes}\\\\
&= {\dfrac{5}{2}}\text{ minutes} \times \dfrac{60\text{ seconds}}{1\text{ minute}}\\\\
&= {\dfrac{5}{2}}\cancel{\text{ minutes}} \times \dfrac{60\text{ seconds}}{1\cancel{\text{ minute}}}\\\\
&={\dfrac{{300\text{ seconds}}}{2}}\\\\
&={300\text{ seconds}} \div {2}\\\\
&=\blueD{150}\text{ seconds}\\\\
\end{align}$

Now that both measures are in seconds, we can compare them.

Which is larger? $\greenD{160}$ seconds or $\blueD{150}$ seconds?
$\greenD{160}\text{ seconds}>\blueD{150}\text{ seconds}$
$\greenD{160} \text{ seconds}> \blueD{2\dfrac12}\text{ minutes}$
